item.title: 美国液化天然气生产商几乎无法立即替代卡塔尔失去的货运量。
item.description: 路透社计算及行业分析师指出，在中东冲突导致卡塔尔暂停液化天然气生产后，美国几乎没有闲置产能可迅速提升产量以弥补供应缺口。【原文：The U.S. has little spare capacity to quickly lift output of ​liquefied natural gas and offset lost supply after Qatar halted production due to the conflict in the Middle East, according to Reuters calculations and industry analysts.】

item.title: 对某些小企业而言，关税退税的麻烦不值得为此费心。
item.description: 美国最高法院推翻关税的那天，伊恩·罗森伯格经营的背包公司"日晷"因此损失了数万美元。朋友们纷纷发来祝贺短信，但他并未分享这份喜悦。【原文：The day the U.S. Supreme Court struck down the tariffs that had cost Ian Rosenberger's backpack company Day Owl tens of thousands of dollars, friends started texting him their ​congratulations. He didn't share their elation.】

item.title: 特朗普将在中期选举前与科技巨头会晤，讨论能源承诺事宜
item.description: 美国总统唐纳德·特朗普将于周三会见谷歌、Meta和OpenAI等主要科技公司领导人，旨在正式达成一项承诺，保护消费者免受因能源密集型数据中心快速扩张而导致的电费上涨影响。【原文：U.S. President Donald Trump is set to meet on Wednesday with leaders of major technology ‌companies, including Google , Meta and OpenAI, to formalize a pledge aimed at protecting consumers from rising electricity costs tied to the rapid expansion of energy‑intensive data centers.】

item.title: 杰富瑞因向破产的MFS和First Brands提供贷款面临审查
item.description: 杰富瑞金融集团因英国贷款机构Market Financial Solutions和美国汽车零部件供应商First Brands涉嫌欺诈倒闭，其贷款标准和风险偏好正面临质疑。【原文：Jefferies Financial Group is facing concerns about its lending standards and risk appetite after the collapses of British lender Market Financial Solutions and U.S. auto-parts supplier First Brands from alleged fraud.】

item.title: 美联储哈马克支持维持利率稳定，并向《纽约时报》表示评估伊朗战争影响为时尚早
item.description: 克利夫兰联邦储备银行行长贝丝·哈马克在周三发表的《纽约时报》专访中表示，目前评估伊朗战争的经济影响为时尚早，并支持在"相当长一段时间内"维持利率不变。【原文：Federal Reserve Bank of Cleveland President Beth Hammack said it ​was too early to gauge the economic impact of ‌the Iran war and backed holding interest rates steady for "quite some time", in an interview with the New York Times published ​on Wednesday.】

item.title: 阿迪达斯首席商务官称以色列门店遭炸弹袭击
item.description: 阿迪达斯首席商务官周三表示，该公司位于以色列的一家特许经营店遭遇炸弹袭击，但无人受伤。此次事件发生在伊朗局势持续动荡的背景下。【原文：Adidas' chief commercial officer said ​on Wednesday that one of the company's franchise stores in Israel ‌had been hit by a bomb, but no one had been injured, amid the unfolding war in Iran.】

item.title: 外汇策略师称：自对伊朗开战以来美元的飙升可能不会持久——路透社调查
item.description: 路透社调查的外汇策略师指出，由于市场对美国资产避险属性的疑虑犹存，自美以对伊战争爆发以来美元的反弹可能昙花一现。这些策略师普遍预期美联储今年晚些时候仍将两次降息。【原文：The U.S. dollar's bounceback since the start of the U.S.-Israel war with Iran may be short-lived due to lingering doubts about the safe-haven appeal of U.S. assets, according to FX strategists polled by Reuters who broadly still expect two Federal Reserve rate cuts later this year.】

item.title: 巴西警方拘留了Banco Master所有者Vorcaro，消息人士称
item.description: 巴西商人丹尼尔·沃卡罗——贷款机构Banco Master的拥有者——于周三被联邦警察拘留，这是针对该银行调查的新阶段，一位知情人士透露。【原文：Brazilian businessman Daniel Vorcaro, ‌the owner of lender Banco Master, was detained by federal police on ​Wednesday in a new phase ​of an investigation into the ⁠bank, a person familiar with ​the matter said.】

item.title: 2026年，印尼遭遇一系列市场情绪挫折
item.description: 惠誉周三将印尼主权信用评级展望从稳定下调至负面，这是东南亚最大经济体投资者信心遭受的一系列打击中的最新一击。【原文：Fitch downgraded Indonesia's sovereign credit rating outlook from stable to negative on Wednesday, the latest in a series of blows to investor sentiment in Southeast ​Asia's largest economy.】

item.title: 美以空袭后，中国企业伊朗项目前景不明
item.description: 数据显示，在美以对伊朗发动空袭数周前，大批中国国有企业正瞄准从能源、重工业到贸易促进等领域的项目，凸显出这场战争对中国企业构成的风险。【原文：Weeks before U.S.–Israeli strikes in Iran, wide swathes of state-backed Chinese companies were eyeing projects in areas from energy and heavy industry to trade promotion, data showed, spotlighting ​the risks the war presents for business in China.】

item.title: 欧盟文件显示，Stellantis、丰田和斯巴鲁未被纳入特斯拉2026年碳排放池
item.description: 欧盟文件显示，斯泰兰蒂斯、丰田和斯巴鲁尚未加入由特斯拉主导的本年度碳信用额"共享池"。该机制旨在帮助汽车制造商达成欧盟碳排放目标。【原文：Stellantis Toyota and Subaru have not signed up to the current year's carbon‑credit 'pool' led by Tesla which is designed to ​help carmakers meet the European Union's carbon emissions targets, an ‌EU filing showed.】

item.title: 欧洲央行博客指出，当前人工智能可能正在创造而非消灭就业岗位
item.description: 欧洲央行周三在一篇博客文章中指出，企业日益广泛地使用人工智能技术，可能正在欧元区创造就业岗位，而非如许多人担忧的那样导致岗位流失。【原文：The increasing use of artificial intelligence by firms may be creating some jobs in the euro zone ​rather than destroying them as many fear, a ‌European Central Bank blog post argued on Wednesday.】

item.title: 航空股抛售压力缓解，部分航班因伊朗冲突撤离海湾地区
item.description: 周三，中东地区将有少量遣返航班起飞，各国政府正努力将数万名滞留海外的公民接回国。与此同时，尽管美国和以色列对伊朗的空袭行动升级，全球航空股的抛售潮已有所缓解。【原文：A handful of ​repatriation flights were due to take off from the Middle East on Wednesday as governments try to ‌bring tens of thousands of stranded citizens home, while the selloff in global airline shares eased even as the U.S. and Israeli air war against Iran escalated.】

item.title: 制药商Aspen今年计划在撒哈拉以南非洲地区推进Mounjaro的上市审批
item.description: 阿斯本制药公司首席执行官周三表示，该公司计划今年内为礼来公司的重磅减肥药Mounjaro争取到撒哈拉以南非洲地区的上市许可，以满足该地区对肥胖治疗药物激增的需求。【原文：Aspen Pharmacare aims to secure sub‑Saharan African approval for Eli Lilly's blockbuster weight‑loss drug Mounjaro as early as this year, its CEO said on Wednesday, capitalising on soaring regional ​demand for obesity treatments.】

item.title: 独家：消息人士称，日美计划将核电项目纳入5500亿美元投资计划
item.description: 知情人士周三向路透社透露，日本与美国正努力将核电项目纳入日本5500亿美元投资计划的第二轮协议中。【原文：Japan ​and the United States are working to include a nuclear power project in the second ‌round of deals under Japan's $550-billion investment package, two people with knowledge of the matter told Reuters on Wednesday.】

item.title: 沃尔沃汽车12月至2月销量下滑10%
item.description: 瑞典沃尔沃汽车公司周三表示，今年前三个月（截至2月）共售出156,965辆汽车，较去年同期下降10%，但纯电动汽车销量增长了18%。【原文：Sweden-based Volvo Cars said ‌on Wednesday it sold 156,965 cars in the three months through ​February, down 10% from ​the same period a year ⁠earlier, but sales of fully ​electric cars increased 18%.】

item.title: Belgian museum, US mining company at odds over colonial-era Congo archive
item.description: A U.S. mining company backed by billionaires Jeff Bezos and ​Bill Gates is in a tangle with Belgium's AfricaMuseum over who should digitise antique maps of what is now ‌the Democratic Republic of Congo in the museum's archive.

item.title: Wall Street futures slip on Middle East conflict, oil-driven inflation concerns
item.description: U.S. stock index futures edged lower on Wednesday as investors assessed an ‌escalating Middle East conflict and awaited a fresh slate of economic data for cues on the interest rate outlook, while surging oil prices added to inflation pressures.

item.title: Goldman Sachs raises Q2 Brent oil price forecast by $10 to $76 a barrel
item.description: Goldman Sachs raised on Wednesday its second-quarter 2026 average price forecast for Brent crude ​oil by $10 to $76 per barrel and for WTI by $9 ‌to $71.

item.title: Traton sees better truck sales development in 2026 after last year's drop
item.description: 大众汽车旗下卡车部门特拉顿周三预测，2026年卡车销量将实现增长，销售回报率将与去年基本持平。【原文：Volkswagen's truck unit ​Traton on Wednesday forecast improved unit sales ‌development for 2026, with return on sales broadly on par with last year's number.】

item.title: Bayer projects up to 10.1 bln eur in adj earnings this year
item.description: German pharmaceuticals and crop protection ​group Bayer said ‌on Wednesday it is targeting adjusted earnings of ​up to ​10.1 billion euros ($11.7 billion) this ⁠year, when excluding ​the effect of ​currency swings.

item.title: 意大利金融科技公司Fineco宣布，将在2029年前通过人工智能技术推动新客户增长。
item.description: 意大利数字银行及资产管理公司Fineco周三表示，将通过人工智能技术扩大客户群并削减成本，该战略将持续至2029年，旨在大幅提升新资金的获取速度。【原文：Italian digital bank and money manager ​Fineco said on Wednesday ‌it would use artificial intelligence to expand its client ​base and cut costs ​under a strategy through ⁠2029, aiming to sharply ​grow the pace at ​which it gathers new money.】

item.title: Exclusive: Walmart-backed PhonePe targets up to $10.5 billion valuation in India IPO
item.description: 沃尔玛支持的印度金融科技公司PhonePe——该国使用率最高的支付平台——正计划以90亿至105亿美元估值上市，两位知情人士透露。【原文：Walmart-backed Indian fintech firm PhonePe , the country's most used payments ​platform, is aiming to list at a valuation of between $9 billion and $10.5 billion, two people with direct ‌knowledge of the matter said.】

item.title: 阿迪达斯预计2026年营业利润将升至23亿欧元
item.description: German sportswear maker Adidas on Wednesday ​said it expected its operating profit to ‌increase to around 2.3 billion euros ($2.7 billion) this year, despite around a 400-million-euro impact from U.S. tariffs and ​unfavourable currency developments.

item.title: 大陆集团预测轮胎销售将保持总体稳定，2026年实现盈利
item.description: 德国汽车零部件供应商大陆集团周三表示，在需求持续波动的环境下，其核心轮胎业务2026年的销售额和盈利能力将保持总体稳定。【原文：German car ​parts supplier Continental on Wednesday guided for broadly stable ‌2026 sales and profitability in its core tyres business in a persistently volatile demand environment.】

item.title: 阿迪达斯提名纳赛夫·萨维里斯担任新董事长，并将首席执行官合同延长至2030年
item.description: 阿迪达斯周三宣布，提议由董事会成员纳塞夫·萨维里斯接替托马斯·拉贝担任公司新任董事长。【原文：Adidas is proposing board ‌member Nassef Sawiris as its new chairman, taking over from Thomas Rabe, ​the sportswear company said on ​Wednesday.】

item.title: 焦点：汉莎航空掌门人祭出危机应对方案，应对成本压力与工会斗争
item.description: 在俯瞰法兰克福繁忙国际机场的汉莎航空基地，首席执行官卡斯滕·施波尔凝视着他庞大的航空帝国。巨型屏幕上不断闪烁着航班延误数据及其他运营指标。【原文：At Lufthansa's base overlooking Frankfurt's bustling international airport, chief executive Carsten Spohr has his eye firmly on his sprawling airline empire as giant screens flash flight delay data and other performance indicators in the ​background.】

item.title: 澳大利亚某州计划赋予员工在家办公的法定权利
item.description: 澳大利亚维多利亚州周三宣布，将推出立法赋予员工每周在家办公两天的法定权利。【原文：The Australian state of Victoria said on ​Wednesday it will launch legislation ‌to give employees the legal right to work from home two days ​per week.】

item.title: 德国工会力争在特斯拉柏林工厂实现突破
item.description: 德国最大的工业工会正努力在柏林郊外的特斯拉超级工厂争取更多话语权。该工厂员工正就新一届工人委员会展开投票，此前一场充满恶意攻击和法律挑战的竞选活动已告一段落。【原文：Germany's top industrial union is fighting for more influence at Tesla's gigafactory outside Berlin, where ​staff are voting for a new works council after ‌a campaign marked by mud-slinging and legal challenges.】

item.title: 消息人士称，OpenAI正考虑与北约签订合同
item.description: 知情人士周二透露，OpenAI正考虑与北大西洋公约组织（NATO）签订合同，将其人工智能技术部署在该组织的"非机密"网络中。此前几天，这家ChatGPT母公司刚与五角大楼达成协议。【原文：OpenAI is considering a contract to deploy its AI technology on North Atlantic Treaty ​Organization's (NATO) "unclassified" networks, a person familiar with the matter said on Tuesday, days after the ‌ChatGPT-owner struck a deal with the Pentagon.】
